project.name: minora
project.description: A production-ready Telegram message receiver system built with clean architecture principles that automatically responds to messages and saves links to a database.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment variable template defining required configuration for Telegram bot credentials, Redis connection URL, logging level, and Gemini AI API keys.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker build using Python 3.10-slim that installs Poetry dependencies, copies application code, and starts the FastAPI application on port 8000 with a health check.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Poetry for dependency management inside Docker with virtualenv creation disabled so packages install to system Python.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project documentation for Minora covering clean architecture design, setup instructions, API endpoints, environment configuration, and Docker deployment across 32 sections.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Root package initializer for the minora application, marking the top-level app directory as a Python package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the application interfaces layer.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the application services layer.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the application use cases layer.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the domain entities layer.

nodes.name: llm_provider.py
nodes.summary: Abstract interface defining the contract for LLM providers with async methods for generating structured responses and plain text.

nodes.name: messenger.py
nodes.summary: Abstract interface defining the contract for messenger implementations with an async send method for delivering messages to chat recipients.

nodes.name: queue.py
nodes.summary: Abstract interface defining the contract for message queue implementations with async publish and consume methods for event-driven processing.

nodes.name: command_dispatcher.py
nodes.summary: Application service that routes command execution requests to registered handlers by looking up the command registry and invoking the appropriate handler.

nodes.name: handle_command.py
nodes.summary: Application use case that receives a parsed Command and CommandContext, initializes command setup, and delegates execution to CommandDispatcher.

nodes.name: handle_message.py
nodes.summary: Central message-handling use case that determines whether an incoming Telegram message is a command or regular text, delegating to HandleCommandUseCase or the message service accordingly.

nodes.name: import_source.py
nodes.summary: Use case that imports content from external sources such as Telegram channels or URLs, converts them to KnowledgeDocument entities, and persists them to the knowledge repository.

nodes.name: ingestion_orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a multi-agent content ingestion pipeline for URLs, sequencing fetch, parse, extract, concept, and storage agents with LLM support to produce structured knowledge entries.

nodes.name: knowledge_item.py
nodes.summary: Use case providing full CRUD and search operations on individual knowledge graph nodes including get, update, delete, find, visualize, and content replace actions.

nodes.name: save_knowledge_item.py
nodes.summary: Use case that persists KnowledgeDocument entities to both markdown file storage and the knowledge graph repository, supporting save, delete, and replace operations.

nodes.name: save_link.py
nodes.summary: Use case that extracts and saves URL links found in Telegram messages to persistent storage via the domain link service.

nodes.name: web_knowledge.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive web-facing use case exposing full knowledge graph management including node/edge CRUD, statistics, tag listing, and link management for the REST API layer.

nodes.name: command.py
nodes.summary: Domain entity representing a parsed bot command with its name, argument list, and Pydantic-based validation rules.

nodes.name: command_context.py
nodes.summary: Domain entity holding execution context for a command, including the originating message and associated chat and user metadata.

nodes.name: knowledge.py
nodes.summary: Core domain entities for the knowledge graph, defining KnowledgeEdge (typed relationships between nodes) and KnowledgeDocument (rich knowledge item with metadata and frontmatter serialization).

nodes.name: message.py
nodes.summary: Domain entity representing an incoming Telegram message with sender ID, chat ID, text content, and timestamp fields.

nodes.name: pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Data transfer objects for the multi-stage content ingestion pipeline, defining result types for fetch, parse, extract, concept, and insight stages plus the shared PipelineContext.

nodes.name: LLMProviderInterface
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the interface for LLM providers, declaring async generate (structured) and generate_text (plain string) methods.

nodes.name: Messenger
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for messenger implementations, declaring an async send method for dispatching messages to chat recipients.

nodes.name: Queue
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for message queue implementations, declaring async publish and consume methods for event-driven processing.

nodes.name: CommandDispatcher
nodes.summary: Service that accepts a Command and CommandContext, locates the matching handler in the command registry, and invokes it asynchronously.

nodes.name: HandleCommandUseCase
nodes.summary: Use case that initializes command setup, parses the incoming command, and delegates execution to CommandDispatcher.

nodes.name: HandleMessageUseCase
nodes.summary: Central message processing use case routing incoming Telegram messages to command handling or general message service processing based on content.

nodes.name: ImportSourceUseCase
nodes.summary: Use case that detects content platform, generates source IDs, creates KnowledgeDocument entities from external sources, and persists them to the knowledge repository.

nodes.name: IngestionOrchestrator
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full URL content ingestion pipeline by sequencing fetch, parse, extract, concept, and storage agents with LLM support.

nodes.name: KnowledgeItemUseCase
nodes.summary: Provides comprehensive CRUD operations on knowledge graph nodes including retrieval, update, deletion, search, visualization, and markdown-backed content replacement.

nodes.name: SaveKnowledgeItemUseCase
nodes.summary: Persists KnowledgeDocument entities to markdown storage and the graph repository, handling serialization and supporting delete and replace operations.

nodes.name: SaveLinkUseCase
nodes.summary: Use case that extracts URLs from message content and persists them via the domain link service.

nodes.name: WebKnowledgeUseCase
nodes.summary: Comprehensive use case for web API knowledge graph management exposing node CRUD, edge management, statistics, tag listing, and link operations with pagination and filtering support.

nodes.name: Command
nodes.summary: Pydantic domain entity representing a parsed bot command with name, argument list, and validation logic.

nodes.name: CommandContext
nodes.summary: Domain entity encapsulating execution context for a command, bundling the originating message and user/chat metadata.

nodes.name: KnowledgeEdge
nodes.summary: Domain entity representing a directed typed relationship between two knowledge nodes, carrying type and weight attributes.

nodes.name: KnowledgeDocument
nodes.summary: Core domain entity for a knowledge graph node, holding title, content, tags, type, status, edges, and frontmatter serialization for markdown persistence.

nodes.name: Message
nodes.summary: Domain entity representing an incoming Telegram message with sender ID, chat ID, text content, and timestamp.

nodes.name: FetchResult
nodes.summary: Data transfer object carrying raw fetched content and metadata from the pipeline fetch stage.

nodes.name: ParseResult
nodes.summary: Data transfer object holding structured parsed content produced by the pipeline parse stage.

nodes.name: ExtractResult
nodes.summary: Data transfer object containing extracted entities and facts from the pipeline extract stage.

nodes.name: ConceptResult
nodes.summary: Data transfer object holding concept generation output from the LLM concept agent pipeline stage.

nodes.name: InsightEntry
nodes.summary: Data transfer object representing a single insight produced during pipeline processing.

nodes.name: InsightResult
nodes.summary: Data transfer object aggregating a collection of InsightEntry objects from the insight stage.

nodes.name: PipelineContext
nodes.summary: Shared context object passed between pipeline stages, accumulating fetch, parse, extract, concept, and insight results alongside the source URL and metadata.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer that marks the enums directory as a Python package and exposes its submodules.

nodes.name: message_type.py
nodes.summary: Defines the MessageType enum enumerating the recognized categories of incoming Telegram messages (e.g. command, link, text).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer that marks the domain services directory as a Python package.

nodes.name: command_parser.py
nodes.summary: Domain service that detects whether a Telegram message is a bot command and parses the command name and arguments from the message text.

nodes.name: link_service.py
nodes.summary: Domain service that extracts, validates, and processes URLs from message text, providing helpers for domain extraction and link-response generation.

nodes.name: message_service.py
nodes.summary: Domain service that generates automated reply text based on incoming message content and type.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the infrastructure agents sub-package; exposes the agents module to importers.

nodes.name: base_agent.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract BaseAgent class implementing the template-method pattern for all pipeline agents, with a public run() method and abstract _execute() hook.

nodes.name: concept_agent.py
nodes.summary: Pipeline agent that builds concept knowledge-graph nodes from LLM-extracted topics, normalizing slugs and persisting concept documents.

nodes.name: extract_agent.py
nodes.summary: Pipeline agent that uses an LLM to extract structured metadata (title, author, tags, entities) from parsed web-page content.

nodes.name: fetch_agent.py
nodes.summary: Pipeline agent that fetches raw HTML content from a URL with configurable retries and timeout handling.

nodes.name: insight_agent.py
nodes.summary: Pipeline agent that sends extracted knowledge to an LLM to generate analytical insights and persists them as insight documents.

nodes.name: parse_agent.py
nodes.summary: Pipeline agent that cleans and parses raw HTML into structured text, extracting title, author, description, language, and main content.

nodes.name: report_agent.py
nodes.summary: Pipeline agent that aggregates pipeline results into a formatted summary report for delivery back to the user.

nodes.name: storage_agent.py
nodes.summary: Pipeline agent that persists source and knowledge documents to the knowledge repository, converting extracted data to Markdown and building graph node records.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the commands sub-package; re-exports the setup module to make command registration accessible.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer that marks the command handlers directory as a Python sub-package.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Telegram command handlers for settings management and import/export operations, including an async pipeline that imports external link data.

nodes.name: data.py
nodes.summary: Telegram command handlers for CRUD operations on stored link data, including list, find, read, update, delete, clear, visualize, and history commands.

nodes.name: system.py
nodes.summary: Telegram command handlers for system-level commands (help, status, ping, start, whoami, version) with a factory for the dynamic help handler.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Defines CommandRegistry, a container that stores command metadata and handler functions, supporting lookup, filtering by category, and help-message formatting.

nodes.name: setup.py
nodes.summary: Wires all command handlers into a CommandRegistry and exposes a cached get_command_registry() factory used by the application layer.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic-based Settings class that loads all application configuration (Telegram token, database URL, Gemini API keys, agent tuning) from environment variables.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Database lifecycle utilities: applies Alembic migrations on startup, initialises SQLAlchemy engine/session factory, and provides per-request session generators.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model definitions for all database tables: LinkRecord, NodeRecord, EdgeRecord, TagRecord, NodeTagRecord, EmbeddingRecord, and metadata tables for knowledge graph entities.

nodes.name: MessageType
nodes.summary: Enum that classifies Telegram messages into distinct types such as COMMAND, LINK, and TEXT for downstream routing.

nodes.name: CommandParser
nodes.summary: Parses raw Telegram message text to identify bot commands and split them into name and argument components.

nodes.name: LinkService
nodes.summary: Extracts and validates URLs from text, derives domain names, and generates structured link response objects.

nodes.name: MessageService
nodes.summary: Generates automated reply text for incoming Telegram messages based on message type and content.

nodes.name: BaseAgent
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for all knowledge-pipeline agents; implements the template-method pattern with a public run() that wraps the abstract _execute() hook with error handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python ABCMeta with @abstractmethod to enforce _execute() implementation in subclasses.

nodes.name: ConceptAgent
nodes.summary: Agent that takes LLM-extracted concept topics and creates or updates concept nodes in the knowledge graph with normalised slugs and metadata.

nodes.name: ExtractAgent
nodes.summary: Agent that prompts an LLM to extract structured information (title, tags, entities, summary) from web-page text.

nodes.name: FetchAgent
nodes.summary: Agent that performs HTTP GET requests to retrieve raw web-page HTML, with configurable headers, timeouts, and retry logic.

nodes.name: InsightAgent
nodes.summary: Agent that sends article content and metadata to an LLM to derive analytical insights and persists them as insight knowledge documents.

nodes.name: ParseAgent
nodes.summary: Agent that parses raw HTML using BeautifulSoup, removing noise and extracting structured fields (title, author, description, language, main body text).
nodes.languageNotes: Implements multiple private _extract_* helpers for each metadata field, following the single-responsibility principle within the agent.

nodes.name: ReportAgent
nodes.summary: Agent that assembles a human-readable pipeline report from all intermediate results, used to send a final summary message back through Telegram.

nodes.name: StorageAgent
nodes.summary: Agent that persists source documents and knowledge-graph nodes to the repository, converting structured data to Markdown and mapping to ORM records.

nodes.name: CommandRegistry
nodes.summary: Stores command metadata (name, description, category, visibility) alongside their handler callables and provides lookup, filtering, and help-text generation.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseSettings subclass that reads Telegram bot token, database URL, and Gemini API key configuration from environment variables with helper accessors.

nodes.name: LinkRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for the links table, storing URL, title, domain, tags, and metadata for saved Telegram links with a to_dict serializer.

nodes.name: NodeRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for knowledge-graph node records, storing node type, title, slug, and content.

nodes.name: EdgeRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for knowledge-graph edges, representing directed relationships between two NodeRecord entries.

nodes.name: TagRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for tags, storing unique tag names that can be associated with knowledge graph nodes.

nodes.name: NodeTagRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM join table linking nodes to tags in a many-to-many relationship.

nodes.name: EmbeddingRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model storing vector embeddings for knowledge-graph nodes to support semantic search.

nodes.name: SourceMetadataRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for source-node metadata, storing URL, author, language, and publication date for web articles.

nodes.name: ConceptMetadataRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for concept-node metadata, extending NodeRecord with concept-specific attributes.

nodes.name: InsightMetadataRecord
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for insight-node metadata, linking insights to their source nodes.

nodes.name: handle_settings
nodes.summary: Command handler that returns the current bot settings summary to the Telegram user.

nodes.name: handle_import
nodes.summary: Command handler that accepts a file attachment from Telegram and triggers an async import pipeline to load link records into the database.

nodes.name: _run_import_pipeline
nodes.summary: Internal async helper that processes an imported file, parsing records and persisting them via the database use case.

nodes.name: _run_with_use_case
nodes.summary: Shared helper that wraps a data use-case call with database session management and error handling for all data command handlers.

nodes.name: handle_list
nodes.summary: Command handler that fetches and returns a paginated list of stored link records to the Telegram user.

nodes.name: handle_find
nodes.summary: Command handler that searches stored links by keyword and returns matching records.

nodes.name: handle_visualize
nodes.summary: Command handler that generates and sends a visual representation (chart or graph) of stored link statistics.

nodes.name: build_help_handler
nodes.summary: Factory function that closes over the CommandRegistry to produce a help-command handler which formats and returns the full command listing.

nodes.name: build_registry
nodes.summary: Instantiates a CommandRegistry, registers every command handler (system, data, config categories) with metadata, and returns the configured registry.

nodes.name: get_command_registry
nodes.summary: Cached singleton accessor that lazily builds and returns the application-wide CommandRegistry instance.

nodes.name: _migrate_db
nodes.summary: Runs Alembic migrations programmatically against the target database, applying all pending schema upgrades.

nodes.name: init_db
nodes.summary: Initialises the SQLAlchemy engine and session factory from the configured database URL, then runs migrations.

nodes.name: get_session
nodes.summary: Async generator that yields a SQLAlchemy session for a single request and commits or rolls back on exit.

nodes.name: get_session_maker
nodes.summary: Returns the module-level async session factory, raising an error if the database has not been initialised.

nodes.name: repository.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy-backed `LinkRepository` implementing CRUD operations for persisted links, including save, lookup by URL or chat, and status marking.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the knowledge infrastructure module, re-exporting `MarkdownStorage` for convenient import.

nodes.name: markdown_storage.py
nodes.summary: File-system markdown persistence layer with `MarkdownDocument` and `MarkdownStorage` providing CRUD operations on slug-based `.md` files with YAML frontmatter.

nodes.name: repository.py
nodes.summary: `KnowledgeRepository` providing full graph-aware persistence for knowledge nodes, edges, tags, embeddings, and type-specific metadata using SQLAlchemy.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the LLM infrastructure module.

nodes.name: gemini_provider.py
nodes.summary: Concrete `GeminiProvider` that wraps Google Gemini REST API with key rotation, payload building, and JSON/text response parsing for AI-driven message processing.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements key rotation across multiple API keys to avoid rate-limit exhaustion.

nodes.name: provider.py
nodes.summary: Abstract `LLMProvider` base class and `LLMProviderError` exception defining the interface contract for all LLM backend implementations.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the queue infrastructure module.

nodes.name: redis_queue.py
nodes.summary: `RedisQueue` implementing async pub/sub message queuing via Redis, with connect/disconnect lifecycle and publish/consume operations.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the Telegram infrastructure module.

nodes.name: polling.py
nodes.summary: `TelegramPoller` implementing long-polling against Telegram Bot API, normalizing incoming updates into domain message entities and dispatching them for processing.

nodes.name: telegram_messenger.py
nodes.summary: `TelegramMessenger` providing a thin HTTP wrapper for sending Telegram messages and retrieving bot updates via the Bot API.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the interface layer.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer for the API interface sub-package.

nodes.name: web_ui.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router providing the REST API for the knowledge-graph web UI, including endpoints for nodes, edges, tags, and links, backed by the `WebKnowledge` use-case.

nodes.name: webhook.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router exposing the Telegram webhook endpoint, normalizing incoming updates and delegating to the `HandleMessage` use-case.

nodes.name: logging_config.py
nodes.summary: Configures structured logging for the application with adjustable log levels and formatter setup via `setup_logging`.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application factory and lifespan manager, wiring all routers, initialising the database, and exposing the ASGI `app` object as the server entry point.

nodes.name: api.js
nodes.summary: Frontend API client module providing fetch wrappers for every backend REST endpoint consumed by the knowledge-graph SPA.

nodes.name: app.js
nodes.summary: SPA router module managing hash-based navigation, view rendering dispatch, and active nav-link highlighting.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Single-page application shell HTML loading all static JS/CSS assets and providing the top-level nav and main content container.

nodes.name: style.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS stylesheet for the knowledge-graph SPA, defining layout, typography, component styles, and responsive utilities.

nodes.name: ui.js
nodes.summary: Shared UI utility functions for the SPA, including toast notifications, modal management, HTML escaping, date formatting, pagination rendering, and markdown rendering.

nodes.name: view_dashboard.js
nodes.summary: Dashboard view renderer that fetches knowledge-graph statistics and renders summary metrics and distribution charts.

nodes.name: view_detail.js
nodes.summary: Node detail view renderer that fetches a single knowledge node by ID and displays its metadata, content, edges, and tags.

nodes.name: LinkRepository
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy repository implementing persistence for `Link` entities with methods for save, URL lookup, chat-scoped listing, and processed-status update.

nodes.name: MarkdownDocument
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing an in-memory markdown document with frontmatter metadata and body content, providing a `to_frontmatter` serialisation helper.

nodes.name: MarkdownStorage
nodes.summary: File-system storage engine for markdown documents: handles path building, slugification, hash computation, and CRUD operations on `.md` files.

nodes.name: KnowledgeRepository
nodes.summary: Comprehensive SQLAlchemy repository for the knowledge graph, managing nodes, edges, tags, embeddings, and type-specific metadata with upsert semantics.

nodes.name: GeminiProvider
nodes.summary: Concrete LLM provider integrating with Google Gemini via REST, with multi-key rotation, configurable safety settings, and structured JSON response extraction.

nodes.name: LLMProvider
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the `generate` and `generate_text` interface contract that all LLM provider implementations must satisfy.

nodes.name: LLMProviderError
nodes.summary: Custom exception class for LLM provider failures, carrying a message and an optional underlying cause.

nodes.name: RedisQueue
nodes.summary: Redis-backed message queue providing async connect/disconnect lifecycle with publish and consume operations for inter-service messaging.

nodes.name: TelegramPoller
nodes.summary: Long-polling Telegram bot client that fetches updates, normalises them into domain `Message` entities, and dispatches them to the application layer.

nodes.name: TelegramMessenger
nodes.summary: HTTP wrapper for the Telegram Bot API providing `send` and `get_updates` methods with retry and error handling.

nodes.name: NodeCreateRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model for creating a knowledge node, capturing type, title, content, URL, tags, and optional metadata.

nodes.name: NodeUpdateRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model for partially updating a knowledge node's title, content, URL, and tags.

nodes.name: ContentReplaceRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model carrying the new markdown content body for a full content-replacement operation.

nodes.name: EdgeCreateRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model for creating a directed edge between two knowledge nodes with a typed relationship label.

nodes.name: LinkUpdateRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model for updating a link's processed status and associated knowledge-node reference.

nodes.name: list_nodes
nodes.summary: FastAPI route handler returning a paginated, filterable list of knowledge nodes with tag counts.

nodes.name: update_node
nodes.summary: FastAPI route handler performing partial update on a knowledge node's mutable fields.

nodes.name: replace_content
nodes.summary: FastAPI route handler replacing a knowledge node's full markdown content body.

nodes.name: list_links
nodes.summary: FastAPI route handler listing all persisted links with optional filter by processed status.

nodes.name: update_link
nodes.summary: FastAPI route handler updating a link's processed flag and optional knowledge-node association.

nodes.name: initialize_webhook
nodes.summary: Registers the Telegram webhook URL with the Bot API on application startup using the configured bot token and public base URL.

nodes.name: normalize_message
nodes.summary: Transforms a raw Telegram update payload into a typed domain `Message` object, handling text, photo, document, audio, and sticker message types.

nodes.name: telegram_webhook
nodes.summary: Main FastAPI webhook handler receiving Telegram updates, normalising the message, invoking the `HandleMessage` use-case, and sending the reply.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Configures Python's root logger with a structured formatter and configurable log level for consistent application-wide logging.

nodes.name: lifespan
nodes.summary: FastAPI lifespan context manager handling database initialisation, webhook registration, and graceful shutdown sequence.

nodes.name: create_app
nodes.summary: Application factory creating and configuring the FastAPI instance: mounts static files, includes all routers, sets up CORS, and returns the ASGI app.

nodes.name: parseHash
nodes.summary: Parses the URL hash fragment into a view name and parameter object for the SPA router.

nodes.name: route
nodes.summary: Dispatches hash-based navigation to the appropriate view renderer (dashboard, detail, or default).

nodes.name: formatDate
nodes.summary: Formats an ISO timestamp string into a human-readable locale date-time string with fallback for null values.

nodes.name: renderPagination
nodes.summary: Renders a pagination control strip with prev/next buttons and a page indicator for list views.

nodes.name: renderDashboard
nodes.summary: Fetches graph statistics from the API and renders the dashboard view including node counts and type-distribution bars.

nodes.name: renderDetail
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders the detail view for a knowledge node, including metadata header, markdown content, edges, and tag list.

nodes.name: view_knowledge.js
nodes.summary: Frontend JavaScript view for the Knowledge Explorer UI, providing paginated listing, search, sorting, and full CRUD (create, edit, delete) operations for knowledge nodes via the REST API.

nodes.name: renderKnowledge
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders the knowledge node table with pagination, sorting, and filtering controls, building the HTML dynamically from API results.

nodes.name: openCreateNodeModal
nodes.summary: Opens a modal dialog with a form for creating a new knowledge node, setting up input fields for title, type, status, and tags.

nodes.name: submitCreateNode
nodes.summary: Submits the create-node form data to the API, then closes the modal and refreshes the knowledge list on success.

nodes.name: openEditNodeModal
nodes.summary: Fetches a knowledge node by ID and opens a pre-filled edit modal, populating all form fields with current node data.

nodes.name: submitEditNode
nodes.summary: Submits edited node data to the API update endpoint, refreshing the view and showing a toast notification on completion.

nodes.name: view_others.js
nodes.summary: Frontend JavaScript view handling three secondary UI sections: Links Manager (CRUD for saved links), Edges viewer (knowledge-graph relationships), and Tags listing.

nodes.name: renderLinks
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders the paginated links table with status filter, search, and edit/delete action buttons for each link.

nodes.name: openEditLinkModal
nodes.summary: Fetches a link record by ID and opens a pre-filled modal for editing URL, title, status, and source type fields.

nodes.name: renderEdges
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders the knowledge-graph edges table showing source/target node relationships with create and delete actions.

nodes.name: renderTags
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders a tag cloud or list of all tags used across knowledge nodes, with clickable filtering.

nodes.name: web_main.py
nodes.summary: Standalone FastAPI application entry point for the Web UI knowledge management interface, loading web config, initializing the database, mounting static files, and wiring up the web router.

nodes.name: _load_web_config
nodes.summary: Reads web configuration from configs/web_config.yaml, merging file values over built-in defaults for host, port, knowledge_root, and items_per_page.

nodes.name: lifespan
nodes.summary: Async context manager managing the Web UI application lifespan, initializing the database on startup and logging shutdown events.

nodes.name: create_web_app
nodes.summary: Factory function that creates and fully configures the FastAPI Web UI instance, including the web router, static file mount, and root index.html handler.

nodes.name: agents.yaml
nodes.summary: Centralised configuration for the agent pipeline, defining LLM provider settings (Gemini), social-platform URL patterns, per-agent tunables (fetch, parse, extract, concept, insight, storage, report), and pipeline flags.

nodes.name: commands.yaml
nodes.summary: Declarative registry of all Telegram bot commands, each with a display name, command string, aliases, description, usage hint, and response template.

nodes.name: web_config.yaml
nodes.summary: Web UI server configuration specifying host, port (8080), knowledge root directory, items per page, and max search results.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Root Pytest configuration file that sets required environment variables (Telegram token, Redis URL, log level, etc.) before any application imports run, ensuring a consistent test environment.

nodes.name: pytest_configure
nodes.summary: Pytest hook that injects all required environment variables at the very earliest configuration stage, preventing import-time failures in tests.

nodes.name: minora.db
nodes.summary: SQLite database file used as the persistent data store for the minora application, holding saved links, knowledge nodes, edges, and tags.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration file defining two services: the main minora application container (built from local Dockerfile, port 8000) and a Redis 7 cache, connected via a private bridge network.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses environment variable substitution for TELEGRAM_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL secrets.

nodes.name: app
nodes.summary: Main minora application service built from the local Dockerfile, exposing port 8000 and depending on the Redis service with restart-unless-stopped policy.

nodes.name: redis
nodes.summary: Redis 7 (Alpine) cache service exposing port 6379 with persistent volume storage on the telegram_network bridge.

nodes.name: 2 layer design.md
nodes.summary: Architectural design document describing the two-layer knowledge storage model: a raw link layer and a structured knowledge-node layer, including data flow, API contracts, and schema decisions.

nodes.name: COMMANDS.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reference for all Telegram bot commands, including usage syntax, alias variants, argument formats, and example responses for each command.

nodes.name: COMMAND_SYSTEM.md
nodes.summary: Technical documentation for the command-handling system architecture, explaining how commands are registered, routed, parsed, and dispatched to handlers.

nodes.name: DATABASE.md
nodes.summary: Database usage guide covering schema overview, ORM models, migration workflow, and query patterns for the minora SQLite store.

nodes.name: FEATURES.md
nodes.summary: High-level feature overview of the minora system, documenting capabilities such as link saving, knowledge extraction, agent pipeline processing, and Web UI functionality.

nodes.name: IMPORT_USAGE.md
nodes.summary: Developer guide explaining import conventions, module structure, and usage patterns for consuming internal packages within the clean-architecture codebase.

nodes.name: POLLING_SETUP.md

layers.name: Domain Layer
layers.description: Pure business logic containing Telegram message entities, command models, knowledge entities, enums, and domain services like link extraction and command parsing — with no external dependencies.

layers.name: Application Layer
layers.description: Use cases orchestrating business workflows (message handling, link saving, knowledge ingestion, command dispatch) and abstract interfaces defining contracts for LLM, messenger, and queue providers.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Concrete implementations of external system adapters including SQLAlchemy database models and repositories, Telegram polling and messaging, Gemini LLM provider, Redis queue, AI agents pipeline, bot command handlers, and the SQLite database file.

layers.name: Interface Layer
layers.description: FastAPI webhook and web UI endpoints, the Telegram bot entry point (main.py), web server entry point (web_main.py), static frontend assets (HTML/CSS/JS dashboards), and application bootstrap configuration.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project-level configuration including environment variable template, YAML configs for agents, commands, and web settings, plus Python project metadata and test runner configuration.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation covering setup guides, architecture walkthroughs, clean architecture design notes, command references, database schema docs, implementation plans, and ingested knowledge source files.

layers.name: Deployment Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker container build definition and Docker Compose orchestration for the bot application and Redis service, enabling containerized production and development deployments.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest-based unit and integration tests covering use cases, domain services, infrastructure adapters (Gemini, Redis), agent pipelines, command routing, and webhook endpoints, with shared fixtures in conftest.py.

layers.name: Developer Tools
layers.description: Developer utility scripts for demonstrating the command system, running end-to-end import tests, and inspecting the SQLite database contents outside of the main application.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand Minora's purpose: a production-ready Telegram bot that automatically responds to messages, detects and saves URLs to a database, and runs a multi-agent AI pipeline to build a local knowledge graph. The README also lays out the four-layer clean architecture — Domain → Application → Infrastructure → Interface — that organizes every file you'll encounter in the tour.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: app/main.py is the ASGI entry point: it creates the FastAPI app, registers all routers, initialises the database on startup, and exposes the `app` object that Uvicorn serves. Reading it top-to-bottom gives you the complete wiring diagram of the system — every layer plugs in here. The companion app/infrastructure/config.py uses Pydantic Settings to pull the Telegram token, database URL, Redis URL, and Gemini API keys from environment variables, validating them at boot time so misconfiguration fails fast.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI's `@asynccontextmanager` lifespan pattern replaces the old `startup`/`shutdown` event hooks: a single async generator function runs setup code before `yield` and teardown code after, giving the app a clean lifecycle that plays nicely with async resource management.

tour.title: Domain Entities
tour.description: The Domain Layer contains zero external dependencies — it is the vocabulary every other layer speaks. `message.py` defines the `TelegramMessage` dataclass (sender ID, chat ID, text, timestamp) that crosses all layer boundaries as the canonical representation of an incoming update. `knowledge.py` defines `KnowledgeDocument` and `KnowledgeEdge`, the rich graph-node types that the AI pipeline ultimately produces. `pipeline.py` defines the DTOs (`FetchResult`, `ParseResult`, `ExtractResult`, `ConceptResult`, `InsightResult`, `PipelineContext`) that carry data between each stage of the multi-agent pipeline.
tour.languageLesson: Python dataclasses with `@dataclass(frozen=True)` or plain `@dataclass` enforce immutability at the domain layer, keeping entities as pure value objects with no behaviour tied to infrastructure concerns.

tour.title: Domain Services
tour.description: Domain services encode business rules that don't belong to a single entity. `link_service.py` extracts and validates URLs from raw message text, strips UTM parameters, and formats the auto-reply — all without touching the database or Telegram API. `command_parser.py` detects whether a message starts with `/` and parses out the command name and arguments. Because both services depend only on domain entities and the Python standard library, they are trivially unit-testable and completely portable.

tour.title: Application Interfaces (Ports)
tour.description: The Application Layer defines abstract interfaces — ports in hexagonal architecture — that describe *what* the system needs from the outside world without specifying *how*. `messenger.py` declares `async send(chat_id, text)`. `llm_provider.py` declares `async generate_structured()` and `async generate_text()`. `queue.py` declares `async publish()` and `async consume()`. Every concrete adapter in the Infrastructure Layer implements one of these three contracts, so the application logic never imports Telegram, Gemini, or Redis directly.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `abc.ABC` and `@abstractmethod` enforce interface contracts at class-definition time. Combining them with type hints makes the expected signature explicit and allows static analysis tools to catch mismatched implementations before runtime.

tour.title: Application Use Cases
tour.description: `handle_message.py` is the central orchestrator for every incoming Telegram update: it calls `command_parser` to decide the message type, then delegates to `HandleCommandUseCase` for `/commands` or to the message service for regular text. `save_link.py` sits alongside it, using the domain link service to extract URLs and persisting them through the repository interface. The `command_dispatcher.py` service connects `HandleCommandUseCase` to the command registry, routing `/help`, `/data`, and `/config` to their registered handlers. Together these use cases encode the complete business workflows described in the README.

tour.title: Telegram Infrastructure Adapter
tour.description: `telegram_messenger.py` is the concrete implementation of the `Messenger` interface: it wraps `httpx` calls to the Telegram Bot API's `sendMessage` endpoint and exposes `get_updates` for polling mode. `polling.py` drives long-polling: it loops against `getUpdates`, maps raw JSON into `TelegramMessage` domain entities, and dispatches them to the `HandleMessage` use case — the same handler that the webhook uses. This dual-mode design (long-polling for local dev, webhooks for production) is described in the README's Quick Start section and enabled by this single abstraction boundary.
tour.languageLesson: Long-polling with `offset = last_update_id + 1` is a stateful technique: by always acknowledging the previous batch of updates, Telegram's servers know which updates have been processed and never re-deliver them, providing at-least-once delivery semantics without a broker.

tour.title: Database Layer
tour.description: `database/models.py` is the most fan-out-heavy file in the codebase (19 outgoing edges), defining SQLAlchemy ORM models for every table: `LinkRecord`, `NodeRecord`, `EdgeRecord`, `TagRecord`, `NodeTagRecord`, and `EmbeddingRecord`. These models represent the full knowledge graph in SQLite. `database/repository.py` implements `LinkRepository` on top of these models, providing the CRUD operations (`save`, `find_by_url`, `find_by_chat`, `mark_processed`) that the `SaveLink` use case depends on. The repository pattern keeps SQL entirely inside the Infrastructure Layer.
tour.languageLesson: SQLAlchemy's `DeclarativeBase` + `Mapped[]` typed columns (SQLAlchemy 2.x style) provide Python-level type safety for ORM models: column types are inferred from the type annotation, and the `mapped_column()` descriptor replaces `Column()`, making model definitions more concise and IDE-friendly.

tour.title: Multi-Agent Ingestion Pipeline
tour.description: The README's standout feature is the multi-agent content ingestion pipeline that transforms a URL into a structured knowledge graph entry. `base_agent.py` defines `BaseAgent` using the template-method pattern: a public `run()` method handles error wrapping and logging, while each concrete agent implements `_execute()`. The pipeline flows Fetch → Parse → Extract → Concept → Storage, with `ingestion_orchestrator.py` sequencing these agents and threading the `PipelineContext` DTO through each stage. `concept_agent.py` calls the LLM provider to extract topics and build graph nodes; `storage_agent.py` persists the results through `KnowledgeRepository`.
tour.languageLesson: The template-method pattern (abstract `_execute()` hook in a concrete `run()` wrapper) is ideal for pipelines where every step shares boilerplate — error handling, logging, timing — but differs only in its core logic. It eliminates copy-paste and ensures uniform observability across all agents.

tour.title: Command System
tour.description: The command system gives the bot a `/help`, `/data`, and `/config` interface. `commands/registry.py` defines `CommandRegistry`, a dict-backed container that stores handler functions keyed by command name and can generate formatted help text by category. `commands/setup.py` wires all handlers into the registry at startup via a cached `get_command_registry()` factory. `commands/handlers/system.py` implements the system commands (`/help`, `/status`, `/ping`, `/whoami`), while sibling files handle `/data` (database queries) and `/config` (runtime settings). The `command_dispatcher.py` from Step 6 routes incoming commands through this registry.

tour.title: Interface Layer: Webhooks & Web UI
tour.description: `interface/api/webhook.py` is the FastAPI router that receives POST requests from Telegram, deserializes the JSON update body into a `TelegramMessage`, and calls `HandleMessage` — the same use case that long-polling drives. `interface/api/web_ui.py` (11 outgoing edges) exposes the REST API for the knowledge-graph dashboard: endpoints for nodes, edges, tags, and links, all backed by the `WebKnowledge` use case. The `app/static/` frontend — `index.html` + `app.js` SPA router + view modules — consumes these endpoints to render the interactive knowledge graph UI described in the README's Features section.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI `APIRouter` objects let you split endpoint definitions across files and mount them onto the main `app` in `main.py` with a prefix. This mirrors the clean-architecture layer boundary: the interface layer owns HTTP concerns, while the use cases own business logic — neither bleeds into the other.

tour.title: Containerization & Deployment
tour.description: The `Dockerfile` packages the application into a Python 3.10-slim image: Poetry installs dependencies, the app source is copied in, and Uvicorn starts on port 8000 with a health check. `docker-compose.yml` orchestrates two services — the `minora` application container and a `redis:7` cache — connected by a private bridge network. The `.env.example` file documents every environment variable needed (Telegram token, webhook URL, Gemini keys, Redis URL) so a new developer can clone the repo, fill in `.env`, and run `docker compose up` to get a fully working production-like environment.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose's `depends_on` with a `condition: service_healthy` health check ensures the bot container only starts after Redis passes its TCP probe — preventing startup-race failures where the app tries to connect to Redis before it is ready to accept connections.
